RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 201-D, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative John Martin, a Republican, proudly represents part of Boone County (District 44) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2024.
Martin holds a degree in Political Science and Economics from Southwest Baptist University and a Master of Theology degree from Midwestern Baptist Seminary. He is the owner of Pro Pumping and Hydrojetting, LLC, and has a strong background in community leadership, having served as a pastor in Hallsville and Columbia.
Outside of his legislative duties, Martin is actively involved in his community. He is a member of the Chamber of Commerce and Boone County Farm Bureau, and he volunteers with the 4-H and FFA programs. He also organizes the Back the Blue Back the Flag Rally and coaches little league baseball, demonstrating his dedication to both local youth and law enforcement.
Martin is happily married to his wife, Lisa, and together they have four children. Family and community are at the heart of his work, and he is committed to serving his constituents in the Missouri House with integrity and care.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions for initiative petitions and referendums
Modifies provisions relating to the ground ambulance service reimbursement allowance tax
Changes a pilot agricultural education program in elementary schools to a statewide program

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies provisions for initiative petitions
Proposes a constitutional amendment that modifies provisions relating to constitutional amendments
